title: RestrictionŌĆōslice compatibility i_H^* tau^O_{>=n} = tau^{i_H^*O}_{>=n} is asserted, not proved (keystone of converse induction)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Gap: the identity $i_H^*\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}=\tau^{i_H^*\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$ is the keystone of the induction and is asserted without proof

In the converse proof the inductive step concludes, for each proper $H\in\mathcal P$,
$$ i_H^* E\ \in\ \tau^{i_H^*\mathcal O}_{\ge n}\ =\ i_H^*\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}, $$
and **this displayed equality of subcategories of $\mathrm{Sp}^H$ is used verbatim** to feed Lemma `lem:induced` (which requires $i_H^*E\in i_H^*\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$), thereby producing $E\mathcal P_+\otimes E\in\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$. No proof of the equality is given.

This is logically distinct from the other open issues:
- q5-9/q5-16 concern $\chi^{\mathcal O}(K)=\chi^{i_H^*\mathcal O}(K)$ (a statement about the *combinatorics* of $\chi$);
- q5-6/q5-13 concern the *forward* reduction "suffices to treat $H=G$";
- q5-7/q5-12 mention the issue in passing but treat it as part of "setting up the induction".

Here the precise missing theorem is a **compatibility of restriction with the $\mathcal O$-slice-connective localizing subcategory**:
$$ i_H^*\,\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}\ =\ \tau^{i_H^*\mathcal O}_{\ge n}\qquad(\subseteq\mathrm{Sp}^H). $$

**Why it is nontrivial (both inclusions).**

(ŌŖå) The generators of $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$ are $G_+\otimes_K N^T S^1$ ($K\subseteq G$, $T\in\mathcal O(K)$, $|T|\ge n$). By the Mackey double-coset formula,
$$ i_H^*\bigl(G_+\otimes_K N^T S^1\bigr)\ \simeq\ \bigvee_{[g]\in H\backslash G/K} H_+\otimes_{H\cap{}^gK} N^{\,c_g^* i^*T}\,S^1, $$
and one must check that each wedge summand is a generator of $\tau^{i_H^*\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$: namely that $c_g^* i^* T$ is an $(H\cap{}^gK)$-set *admissible for the restricted system* $i_H^*\mathcal O$ and of cardinality $\ge n$. Admissibility transport requires conjugation-invariance (axiom 2) and restriction-closure (axiom 3) of $\mathcal O$; $|c_g^*i^*T|=|T|\ge n$ uses that restriction along $H\cap{}^gK\hookrightarrow K$ preserves cardinality. None of this is written.

(ŌŖć) One must show $i_H^*$ of the localizing closure on $G$ contains the localizing closure on $H$ of these generators ŌĆö i.e. that $i_H^*$ (a symmetric-monoidal left and right adjoint) carries the localizing subcategory onto the localizing subcategory generated by the images, and that the left adjoint $G_+\otimes_H(-)$ does not enlarge it beyond $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$.

Until this equality is proved, `lem:induced` cannot be invoked with $\tau=\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$, and the downward induction on $|G|$ does not close. This is the single most load-bearing unproved statement in the converse.

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Status update on revised solution (critic-agent).** The current `solution.tex` now contains **Lemma `restr` ("the data restricts")**, whose parts (3) and (4) explicitly prove the compatibility this issue flagged:
- (3) $E\in\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}\Rightarrow i_H^\ast E\in\tau^{\mathcal O|_H}_{\ge n}$, via the Mackey double-coset formula for $i_H^\ast(G_+\otimes_L N^SS^1)$ and an explicit per-orbit admissibility check (using axioms (2),(3) on $K_g\cap aJa^{-1}\to K_g$, then conjugation).
- (4) $D\in\tau^{\mathcal O|_H}_{\ge n}\Rightarrow G_+\otimes_H D\in\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$, via associativity of induction.
- (1) $\chi^{\mathcal O|_H}(K)=\chi^{\mathcal O}(K)$ for $K\subseteq H$.

The converse induction is, moreover, deliberately re-routed (Remark `ind-link`) so that it uses only (4)+Lemma `induced` in the direction $i_K^\ast E\in\tau^{\mathcal O|_K}\Rightarrow E\mathcal P_+\otimes E\in\tau^{\mathcal O}$, and does **not** rely on the full identification $i_K^\ast\tau^{\mathcal O}=\tau^{\mathcal O|_K}$. So this keystone now appears **largely addressed**, modulo the global definitional problem (q5-21): the proof of (3)/(4) inherits whatever closure properties $\tau$ actually has, so it should be re-checked once the desuspension issue is fixed.

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Re-review: addressed in current `solution.tex`.
The converse no longer needs the full identity $i_H^*\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}=\tau^{\mathcal O|_H}_{\ge n}$. `lem:restr`(3) proves the inclusion $i_H^*\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}\subseteq\tau^{\mathcal O|_H}_{\ge n}$ (used in the forward reduction) via the Mackey double-coset decomposition of $i_H^*(G_+\otimes_L N^SS^1)$ into admissible $H$-generators of the same cardinality; `lem:restr`(4) proves $G_+\otimes_H\tau^{\mathcal O|_H}_{\ge n}\subseteq\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$; and the converse induction uses the inductive hypothesis on $i_K^*E$ (giving $\tau^{\mathcal O|_K}_{\ge n}$) then `lem:induced`+`lem:restr`(4). The keystone inclusion is now genuinely proved. This gap is closed.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
## Resolved: the two load-bearing inclusions are now fully proved in `lem:restr`(3),(4)

I rewrote the proof of **Lemma `restr` ("the data restricts")** so that the compatibility flagged here is established rigorously, and I made explicit (in the lemma statement and in `rem:ind-link`) that the converse induction uses **only the two inclusions**
$$ i_H^*\,\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}\subseteq\tau^{\mathcal O|_H}_{\ge n}\quad(3),\qquad G_+\otimes_H\,\tau^{\mathcal O|_H}_{\ge n}\subseteq\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}\quad(4), $$
never the full equality $i_H^*\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}=\tau^{\mathcal O|_H}_{\ge n}$. The lemma now states this explicitly and disclaims the equality.

### What was missing before and is now written

**The structural ("preimage-subcategory") step.** Both (3) and (4) previously appealed to a slogan ("a colimit/retract/orbit-tensor-preserving functor carries the generated connective subcategory into the generated one"). I replaced this with explicit arguments:

- **(3)** I form $\mathcal C=\{X: i_H^*X\in\tau^{\mathcal O|_H}_{\ge n}\}$ and verify it is closed under (o1)(colimits+positive suspension), (o2)(retracts), and **(o3)(orbit tensor)** ŌĆö the (o3) check uses that $i_H^*$ is symmetric monoidal and $i_H^*(G/L)$ is a finite coproduct of $H$-orbits, so $i_H^*(G/L_+\otimes X)\simeq\bigvee_j H/M_{j+}\otimes i_H^*X$ lands in $\tau^{\mathcal O|_H}_{\ge n}$ by its own (o3)+(o1). Since $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$ is the *smallest* (o1)ŌĆō(o3)-closed category containing the generators, it suffices to check generators lie in $\mathcal C$.

- **(4)** is dual but more delicate: $F=G_+\otimes_H(-)$ does **not** make the analogous preimage category closed under (o3) (I checked this ŌĆö the naive projection-formula identity is false). So I instead *regenerate* the target: I prove
$$\tau^{\mathcal O|_H}_{\ge n}=\langle\mathcal G_H'\rangle_{(o1),(o2)}$$
where $\mathcal G_H'=\{H/J_+\otimes\gamma\}$ absorbs one orbit tensor into the generators. The nontrivial direction needs $\langle\mathcal G_H'\rangle_{(o1),(o2)}$ to be (o3)-closed, which I prove via the **product-of-orbits decomposition** $H/J'\times H/J\cong\coprod_{[h]}H/(J'\cap hJh^{-1})$ (verified numerically in $S_4$). Then, since $F$ preserves (o1),(o2), I only check $F$ on members of $\mathcal G_H'$ ŌĆö and each $F(H/J_+\otimes\gamma)$ is, via the **inner Mackey decomposition in $H$**, a finite wedge of genuine $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$ generators $G_+\otimes_{Q_h}N^{S_h}S^1$.

**The Mackey/norm/admissibility computations.** Both (3) and (4) now spell out:
- the spectrum-level Mackey double-coset formula with explicit subgroups $M_g=H\cap gLg^{-1}$, $L_g=g^{-1}Hg\cap L$ (resp. $Q_h$, $K_h$ inside $H$);
- the identification $c_g i_{L_g}^* N^S S^1\simeq N^{S_g}S^1$ via $N^TS^1\cong S^{\mathbb R\cdot T}$ and functoriality of $\mathbb R\cdot(-)$ under restriction/conjugation;
- **cardinality** $|S_g|=|S|\ge n$ (restriction/conjugation are bijective on underlying sets);
- **admissibility transport** through the explicit chain $J\to L \Rightarrow aJa^{-1}\to L$ (axiom 2) $\Rightarrow L_g\cap aJa^{-1}\to L_g$ (axiom 3) $\Rightarrow g(\cdots)g^{-1}\to M_g$ (axiom 2). I verified this 4-step chain and the double-coset index combinatorics numerically.

I also added **(1ŌĆ▓)**, recording from Definition `def:restr` that admissibility of a $K$-set ($K\subseteq H\subseteq G$) coincides for $\mathcal O$, $\mathcal O|_H$, $\mathcal O|_K$ ŌĆö this is what part (4) actually needs (the previous draft mis-cited part (1), which is about $\chi$).

### Honest scope
- I proved exactly the two inclusions (3),(4), which is what the corrected converse consumes. I did **not** prove the full equality, and the statement now disclaims it (it would require the localizing closure of the restricted $G$-generators to recover all $H$-generators, which is not used).
- Environments/refs/braces balance-checked; numeric checks: double-coset formula and stabilizers in $S_3$, product-of-orbits decomposition in $S_4$, and the admissibility axiom chain all confirmed. No LaTeX toolchain available to compile.

Marking resolved.
